title: Track-status and weather delta enrichment, not just static flags
summary: FastF1 exposes track status, race control messages, and minute-level weather; official F1 strategy coverage explicitly notes Safety Car windows and wet-grip calls as direct pit triggers.
rationale: FastF1 exposes track status, race control messages, and minute-level weather; official F1 strategy coverage explicitly notes Safety Car windows and wet-grip calls as direct pit triggers. The missing signal is probably change dynamics: recent SC/VSC onset, yellow-to-green transitions, rainfall onset, and track-temperature trend.
implementation_hint: Externally join FastF1 session data by race/year/lap window and build lagged features such as current status, laps since SC/VSC started/ended, rain onset within last 1-3 laps, track-temp delta over 1/3/5 minutes, and whether the current lap sits inside a low-cost pit window regime.
expected_effect: Should add lift on rare but high-information pit decisions that current row-wise tyre features cannot explain.
risk: Join quality and lap-time alignment are the main failure modes; broad race-level enrichment without careful timing may add noise.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
